attacking_style: Not a traditional attacker. Focuses on time trials, particularly late-race efforts where form building through three weeks provides advantage over fatiguing specialists.
team_tactics: Minimal team reliance evident. Operates independently in time trials and breakaways, suggesting role as opportunistic stage hunter rather than GC-supported rider.
terrain_preference: Medium-length time trials (15-20km) in Grand Tours where cumulative fatigue neutralizes pure specialists. Shows ability to survive in breakaways but lacks finishing power for sustained attacks.
vulnerability: Limited ceiling in quality breakaways - caught when facing stronger finishers. Unlikely to succeed in early-race time trials against fresh specialists or in pure climbing scenarios.
key_patterns: Builds form progressively through three-week Grand Tours, peaking in final week (2 observations)
Exploits Grand Tour fatigue to beat time trial specialists in late-race ITTs (2 observations)
Can infiltrate quality breakaways but lacks strength to stay away until finish (2 observations)
form_trajectory: negative: 2022 showed reduced capacity, only surviving in breaks rather than winning. Three-year gap suggests declining competitiveness from 2019 peak.
